What would feel different if that part of you were more accepted?

prompts.text: What is something you are tired of being good at?

followUps.text: Who sees that part of you most accurately?
What would feel different if that part of you were more accepted?

prompts.text: What makes you pull away right when a friendship starts getting closer?

followUps.text: What has to be present for that kind of trust to feel possible?
What would you want a friend to understand before you shared it?

prompts.text: What would it take for you to let a friend really help you?

followUps.text: What has to be present for that kind of trust to feel possible?
What would you want a friend to understand before you shared it?

prompts.text: What is something you fear would change how a friend sees you?

followUps.text: What makes that kind of closeness feel safe instead of pressured?
What would it change if you let someone closer there?

prompts.text: When have you tested someone's loyalty instead of trusting it?

followUps.text: What do you protect by holding that back?
How do you know when someone has earned that trust?

prompts.text: What is a need you have in friendship that feels hard to admit?

followUps.text: What are you afraid will be the result if you ask for it?
What would it change if you let someone closer there?

prompts.text: What kind of closeness makes you feel both grateful and exposed?

followUps.text: What could make that kind of closeness not cause you to feel exposed or vulnerable?
What would it change if you let someone closer there?

prompts.text: What is something you want from your friends but rarely give them a chance to offer?

followUps.text: What could a friend do to fill that need?
What would it change if you let someone closer there?

prompts.text: What old friendship still affects the way you trust people?

followUps.text: What part of that memory still feels active in you?
What part of that story are you still making peace with?

prompts.text: Who did you hurt before you understood what you were doing?

followUps.text: What do you understand about it differently now?
What would you tell your younger self about that friendship?

prompts.text: What friendship ending changed how available you let yourself be?

followUps.text: Who were you at that point in your life?
What did it teach you to seek or avoid?

prompts.text: What betrayal still shaped your standards?

followUps.text: Who were you at that point in your life?
What did it teach you to seek or avoid?

prompts.text: What is something you learned to hide because of how people reacted before?

followUps.text: How did that experience shape what you expect from friends?
How does it still affect the way you connect now?

prompts.text: What past rejection do you still try not to repeat?

followUps.text: What do you understand about it differently now?
What would you tell your younger self about that friendship?

prompts.text: What did you once accept in friendship that you would not accept now?

followUps.text: Who were you at that point in your life?
What did it teach you to seek or avoid?

prompts.text: What is a friendship you miss even though you know it could not stay the same?

followUps.text: Who were you at that point in your life?
What has changed that makes this kind of friendship not possible?

prompts.text: What would make you end a friendship even if you still cared about the person?

followUps.text: When has that value been tested in a friendship?
How does that value shape who you stay close to?

prompts.text: What do you think friends owe each other during a crisis?

followUps.text: When has that value been tested in a friendship?

prompts.text: What value do you judge people by more than you admit?
